title: The computer at the bottom of a canal

content_html:
Somewhere at the bottom of the Forth and Clyde Canal, buried in the silt, there is a box of custom silicon that was right about almost everything.

Back in 1988 a Scottish hi-fi company shipped a processor that checked the type and bounds of every memory access in hardware, garbage-collected its own heap in silicon, and treated memory and disk as a single persistent object store. Retellings of the story make it out as charming folly, one where a company that made record turntables decided von Neumann was wrong.

But the story of the Rekursiv deserves to be more than just a curiosity, because almost forty years after it went into the water, its ideas are now shipping in production silicon from Arm; and also because the economics that killed it have just been reversed. Although all these years later, the fine details of the story mostly rest on the recollections of people who were there, it turns out that the hi-fi company were right about almost everything except which decade to build the hardware.

A hi-fi company builds a computer

Linn Products is the Glasgow company Ivor Tiefenbrun founded in 1972, and if you know it at all you know it for the Sondek LP12, still widely regarded by its partisans as the finest record deck ever made.

By the early eighties Linn had built a modern factory at Eaglesham, south of the city itself, and ran their business on a pair of VAX-11/750s along with a pair of 11/780s. But Tiefenbrun came to loathe the software they used. He wanted a system in which every physical object in the factory, down to each individual turntable moving through assembly, test, and even after-sales, had a shadowing software object accumulating its history (Pountain, Byte, November 1988).

So around 1981 Linn hired programmers and a University of Glasgow computer science lecturer, David Harland, and built an object-oriented language called LINGO. LINGO was a Smalltalk with some Algol in its syntax, not to be confused with the other Lingo programming language, which wouldn’t come along for another eight years.

MICRO$COPYTREE: entf 1 pagebus d=ustack
    crtf IDXBADTYPES newtrbr _CONS
    incmsp m.sp' newmptr
    jf MICRO$COPYTREE ldustk d=pgrorr     // the CDR branch
    m.fp 1 uaddbr newmptr
    readustk
    pagebus d=ustack
    idx2 newsr newbr loadaddr
    idxget nocheck incmsp m.sp' newmptr
    jf MICRO$COPYTREE ldustk d=memout     // the CAR branch
    js RTN$CONS
    rtf

Microcode for a recursive tree-copy instruction. Note the instruction calling itself, something forbidden to a conventional CPU (Source: Pountain, 1988).

LINGO worked; but it also ran far too slowly on the VAX to automate anything. Tiefenbrun’s response was pure Tiefenbrun. The hardware was the problem, not the software: so Linn would build hardware.

While stranded on a delayed train home from a seminar, Harland, and another computer engineer Bruno Beloff, sketched the repartitioning of their prototype into custom chips they christened “Rekursiv,” which is why Harland later joked that “the Rekursiv [was] British Rail’s fault.”

A few years later, in 1984, Tiefenbrun formed Linn Smart Computing, installed his brother Marcus as managing director and Harland as technical director, and funded it with Linn’s own money plus roughly £10 million from the Department of Trade and Industry.

LSI Logic fabricated the chips. Four gate arrays in 1.5 micron CMOS, 299 pins each, named NUMERIK, LOGIK, OBJEKT and KLOK. A company that sold the Sondek, the Ittok, and the Asak was never going to name the chips anything else.

Objects all the way down

What made the Rekursiv strange was not that it ran object-oriented programs. It was that a programmer – or, come to that, the compiler – could never see an address. Every object carried a 40-bit number issued at creation, and the OBJEKT chip translated numbers to physical locations through a hashed pager table, checking the type and the bounds of every single access in hardware as it went. Run off the end of an array? The machine refused. Forge a reference? The machine refused. Since only OBJEKT ever knew where anything physically lived, objects could be relocated freely without touching a reference, so garbage collection went into the silicon too: a two-space compacting collector that walked the live objects and slid them into the other half of DRAM while execution carried on above, oblivious.

Persistence worked the same way. Memory and disk were one object store, and if a needed object wasn’t in DRAM the processor simply stalled, mid-instruction, while an external disk processor fetched it, then carried on as if nothing had happened. Because paging sat below the level of instruction execution, a single microcoded instruction could be arbitrarily complex, and could even call itself. That’s the recursion in the name. There was no fixed instruction set at all; the instruction set was a loadable artefact, and you gave the machine whichever one suited your language. Linn supplied C, James Lothian microcoded a Prolog instruction set, a Manchester group put Scheme on it, and Aberdeen ported the persistent language PS-algol. I am a St Andrews alumnus who was around at the right (or if you want to look at it like that, the wrong) time. I can still remember more than I’d want to about PS-algol. But that, as they say, is altogether another story.

The numbers Linn claimed were spectacular. A CONS cell every two microseconds, twenty times the speed of Lisp on a Symbolics workstation, and Prolog unification as a single instruction. But the figures came from Linn’s own simulations, reported in Byte magazine with the company’s cooperation, and nobody ever reproduced them independently.

The attack of the killer micros

The Rekursiv belonged to a respectable intellectual tradition. Through the seventies the industry consensus held that hardware should rise to meet the language, closing what the literature called the semantic gap; Intel bet its first 32-bit design, the object-oriented iAPX 432, on exactly this, and Symbolics built a workstation business on Lisp in microcode.

Unfortunately for the Rekursiv that tradition was demolished in public at almost the exact moment Linn committed to it. Patterson and Ditzel made the case for RISC in 1980: compilers use a fraction of a complex instruction set, the complexity taxes the common case, and simple instructions plus caches plus good compilers go faster. By 1984 Berkeley’s SOAR project had run Smalltalk, the canonical dynamic object language, fast on a simple chip of thirty-five thousand transistors. The Rekursiv’s central assumption was publicly undercut four years before the first board powered on.

Then the economics arrived. Commodity microprocessors improved at roughly 52% a year from 1986 to 2003, the period Eugene Brooks of Lawrence Livermore christened the attack of the killer micros. The Rekursiv took four years to design. It was conceived when a VAX defined respectable performance, and in 1988 it emerged into a world of SPARCstations and 386s, with the 486 arriving close behind.

Lothian, who was there, put it plainly enough five years later: the machine simply couldn’t hold its own against the new workstations. Around twenty or thirty boards were built. Most went to universities. There’s no record that suggests one of them ever ran Linn’s production line, which was the entire point of the exercise. The one field comparison that survives has a threaded-code LINGO on a Sun-3 running about twice as fast as the Rekursiv board it was meant to be inferior to, and which was designed with the intent of running it.

The end was memorably Glaswegian. Black Monday had squeezed Linn’s finances, a venture rescue never closed, and the final straw was a car park. A Linn delivery driver reversed a van into Harland’s Porsche, Tiefenbrun declined to pay for the repair on the grounds that it happened on private ground, and Harland resigned.

On his way out he threw his own accumulated hardware and backup media into the Forth and Clyde Canal. The version of the story where the whole product line goes into the water is better; but the truth is good enough.

At least one complete board escaped, and sits today in the Jim Austin Computer Collection near York.

Linn, meanwhile, recycled the name Numerik for the DAC in its CD range, which is as good a headstone as any. Harland left computing entirely around 1995 and became one of the most prolific historians of spaceflight, several dozen books and counting. As a recovering astronomer myself, I can only approve of the career change.

Right about everything?

But here is why this is all more than an historic anecdote. Because I think we need to take a hard look at the Rekursiv’s four defining design decisions, and then ask where each one stands today.

Hardware memory safety

OBJEKT checked the type and bounds of every access against references the program couldn’t forge. That’s a working description of CHERI, the capability architecture Robert Watson and colleagues have been building at Cambridge and SRI since 2010, in which every pointer carries hardware-enforced bounds, permissions and an unforgeable validity tag.

Arm built it into the Morello prototype boards it shipped in 2022 under the UK’s Digital Security by Design programme, and ships the lighter-weight Memory Tagging Extension in Android phones today.

Microsoft’s security team concluded in 2020 that CHERI would have deterministically mitigated at least two thirds of the memory-safety vulnerabilities it patched in 2019. The idea that the object, with its type and its bounds, is the proper unit of hardware protection has gone from eccentric to frontier.

Garbage collection as an architectural concern

Azul Systems spent the 2000s selling Vega appliances, custom multicore processors with hardware read-barrier support, to make pause-free collection practical for Java heaps; the C4 collector that came out of that work now runs in pure software on commodity x86. The algorithm survived the move; the custom silicon stayed behind.

The single-level persistent store

IBM shipped single-level persistent stores in the System/38 in 1978, carried it through the AS/400, and it survives today as IBM i; Intel spent billions trying to reboot the idea as Optane before winding it down in 2022, and the same ambitions are now migrating into CXL memory fabrics. An idea the market keeps finding reasons to want, and reasons not to pay for, is different from a bad idea.

Silicon shaped to a workload rather than to everything

This is the biggest design decision: the one that is now just the way the industry works. Hennessy and Patterson, the two people most responsible for burying machines like the Rekursiv, spent their Turing Lecture arguing that domain-specific architectures are the only road forward now that Dennard scaling is dead and Moore’s Law is wheezing. Or to be more honest, also dead.

Google’s TPU, Groq’s deterministic streaming processor, Cerebras’s wafer-scale engine and Etched’s transformer ASIC are all machines built to run one computational language well; it just happens that the language is now linear algebra rather than Smalltalk.

Four for four

Every architectural conviction that went into the canal with the Rekursiv is either shipping, in production, at scale, or is the declared direction of an entire field. Four correct calls in a row is too many for luck. The likelier reading is that the almost forty years in between were not the natural state of computing but an accommodation to a scarcity, and that the scarcity has since gone away.

I made a different version of this same argument back in June, about software rather than silicon. In my essay a distribution of one I argued that bespoke software was the original, correct arrangement, that fifty years of productised general-purpose software were a compromise forced by the economics of scarce programmers, and that AI has ended the compromise.

The general-purpose commodity processor was also a compromise, forced by a 52%-a-year improvement curve that made any bespoke architecture obsolete before it reached silicon. That curve is gone. The compromise is going with it; and the workload-shaped machine, the thing Harland was building in 1984, is what comes back.

Where the semantics live

The comfortable lesson, and the one usually made about the Rekursiv, is that you don’t fight the commodity curve. This is true, but no longer very useful, because the curve is dead. The useful lesson is subtler, and you can see it when you think about why IBM succeeded with the same ideas at the same time.

The System/38 had the Rekursiv’s deepest commitments: single-level store, persistent objects addressed by name rather than address, capabilities, everything an object. It thrived for decades because IBM put those semantics in a virtual instruction set, the Technology Independent Machine Interface (TIMI), and translated to whatever processor was underneath. When the platform moved to PowerPC in 1995, customer applications didn’t even need recompiling. Linn welded the same semantics into four specific gate arrays. The abstraction is still running today; the gate arrays lasted five years.

Interestingly enough, Britain has run this experiment more than once, but with very different outcomes.

Acorn, another small British company with contrarian silicon convictions, designed the ARM 1 in 1985; and the business that conquered the world was not Acorn’s computers but the licensing company that sold the abstraction, an instruction set anyone could implement on whatever process node the commodity curve offered next.

Inmos, meanwhile, welded David May’s genuinely brilliant parallel architecture into the Transputer, was outrun by exactly the same curve that took the Rekursiv, and survives today mainly as lineage: May’s XMOS, and Bristol’s continuing habit of contrarian silicon, through to Graphcore. Put the ideas above the silicon and they can outlive any particular chip. Put them in the silicon and they live exactly as long as the process node does.

Everyone needs a canal

The Rekursiv was hardware reshaped to fit a language, because the general-purpose machine fitted the language badly. What’s happening now is the same move in reverse: a whole class of new languages reshaped to fit a machine, because general-purpose languages fit LLMs so poorly. There are enough of them now to fill a catalogue, which I maintain at agentlanguages.dev, where they split into camps that disagree about almost everything except the diagnosis.

Vera, the language I released back in February, is mine. But the instinct underneath all of them is the one Harland was acting on in 1984: that the substrate should change to meet its authors. For him the authors were human and the substrate silicon. This time the authors are models and the substrate the language. Harland made his bet at the worst possible moment in the history of the commodity curve. I’d like to think mine is better timed. The canal is patient either way.

The second time around

Which brings us to why I’m writing about a dead Glaswegian computer.

Our thesis at Negroni has always been that technology waves are cyclical, and that reading the cycle matters more than reading the technology. The Rekursiv is the cleanest case study I know of a team that read the technology almost perfectly and the cycle catastrophically: right about memory safety, right about garbage collection, right about persistence, right about workload-shaped silicon, and wrong about what year it was needed.

The team at Linn were insanely early with a design philosophy which was, to put it politely, exotic at the time. It’s only now, all these years later, that we know they were right.

Judged on its ideas, the Rekursiv holds up embarrassingly well. It’s only judging the execution which makes every decision look wrong. But being right too early is not a business model.

The reason to tell the story now is that the cycle has actually turned. The 52% curve that killed every bespoke architecture of the eighties ended twenty years ago; RISC-V and modern tooling have collapsed the cost of taping out something opinionated; and the government money is even flowing again: DTI millions into Glasgow back then, Digital Security by Design millions into Cambridge and Morello today.

For the first time since Harland stood on that canal bank with a cardboard box full of silicon, a small team with a contrarian conviction about what silicon should do is not automatically doomed by the calendar. That’s precisely why Negroni looks at silicon companies, and when we do, the Rekursiv is the checklist I follow. Is the workload broad enough to pay for the transistors? Do the ideas live above the silicon or welded into it, and does the team know what the compilers will do to them?

Somewhere at the bottom of the Forth and Clyde Canal there’s a computer that was right about almost everything. The ideas behind it took almost forty years to make it back to the river bank, but every one of them has made it to shore. This time around, our job needs to be keeping the silicon out of the water.

sentences.en: Somewhere at the bottom of the Forth and Clyde Canal, buried in the silt, there is a box of custom silicon that was right about almost everything.
sentences.zh: 在福斯和克莱德运河底部的某个地方，埋在淤泥中，有一个定制的硅盒子，几乎所有东西都是正确的。

blocks.html_en: Back in 1988 a Scottish hi-fi company shipped a processor that checked the type and bounds of every memory access in hardware, garbage-collected its own heap in silicon, and treated memory and disk as a single persistent object store. Retellings of the story make it out as charming folly, one where a company that made record turntables decided von Neumann was wrong.

sentences.en: Back in 1988 a Scottish hi-fi company shipped a processor that checked the type and bounds of every memory access in hardware, garbage-collected its own heap in silicon, and treated memory and disk as a single persistent object store.
sentences.zh: 早在 1988 年，一家苏格兰 hi-fi 公司就推出了一款处理器，该处理器可以检查硬件中每次内存访问的类型和范围，在硅中对其自己的堆进行垃圾收集，并将内存和磁盘视为单个持久对象存储。

sentences.en: Retellings of the story make it out as charming folly, one where a company that made record turntables decided von Neumann was wrong.
sentences.zh: 这个故事的重述使它成为一种迷人的愚蠢行为，一家制造唱片转盘的公司认为冯·诺依曼是错误的。

blocks.html_en: But the story of the Rekursiv deserves to be more than just a curiosity, because almost forty years after it went into the water, its ideas are now shipping in production silicon from Arm; and also because the economics that killed it have just been reversed. Although all these years later, the fine details of the story mostly rest on the recollections of people who were there, it turns out that the hi-fi company were right about almost everything except which decade to build the hardware.

sentences.en: But the story of the Rekursiv deserves to be more than just a curiosity, because almost forty years after it went into the water, its ideas are now shipping in production silicon from Arm; and also because the economics that killed it have just been reversed.
sentences.zh: 但 Rekursiv 的故事不只是令人好奇，因为在它下水近 40 年后，它的想法现在正在从 Arm 的生产芯片中发货；还因为扼杀它的经济刚刚发生了逆转。

sentences.en: Although all these years later, the fine details of the story mostly rest on the recollections of people who were there, it turns out that the hi-fi company were right about almost everything except which decade to build the hardware.
sentences.zh: 尽管这么多年过去了，故事的细节大部分都取决于在场人们的回忆，但事实证明，除了硬件的制造年代之外，这家高保真公司几乎所有事情都是正确的。

blocks.html_en: A hi-fi company builds a computer

sentences.en: A hi-fi company builds a computer
sentences.zh: 一家高保真音响公司制造一台计算机

blocks.html_en: Linn Products is the Glasgow company Ivor Tiefenbrun founded in 1972, and if you know it at all you know it for the Sondek LP12, still widely regarded by its partisans as the finest record deck ever made.

sentences.en: Linn Products is the Glasgow company Ivor Tiefenbrun founded in 1972, and if you know it at all you know it for the Sondek LP12, still widely regarded by its partisans as the finest record deck ever made.
sentences.zh: Linn Products 是 Ivor Tiefenbrun 于 1972 年在格拉斯哥成立的公司，如果您了解它，您就会知道 Sondek LP12，至今仍被其支持者广泛认为是有史以来最好的唱片机。

sentences.en: The Linn Sondek LP12
sentences.zh: Linn Sondek LP12

blocks.html_en: By the early eighties Linn had built a modern factory at Eaglesham, south of the city itself, and ran their business on a pair of VAX-11/750s along with a pair of 11/780s. But Tiefenbrun came to loathe the software they used. He wanted a system in which every physical object in the factory, down to each individual turntable moving through assembly, test, and even after-sales, had a shadowing software object accumulating its history (Pountain, Byte, November 1988).

sentences.en: By the early eighties Linn had built a modern factory at Eaglesham, south of the city itself, and ran their business on a pair of VAX-11/750s along with a pair of 11/780s.
sentences.zh: 到了 80 年代初，Linn 在城市南部的 Eaglesham 建造了一家现代化工厂，并在两台 VAX-11/750 和两台 11/780 上开展业务。

sentences.en: But Tiefenbrun came to loathe the software they used.
sentences.zh: 但蒂芬布伦开始厌恶他们使用的软件。

sentences.en: He wanted a system in which every physical object in the factory, down to each individual turntable moving through assembly, test, and even after-sales, had a shadowing software object accumulating its history (Pountain, Byte, November 1988).
sentences.zh: 他想要一个系统，其中工厂中的每个物理对象，直至经过组装、测试甚至售后的每个单独的转盘，都有一个积累其历史记录的影子软件对象（Pountain，Byte，1988 年 11 月）。

blocks.html_en: So around 1981 Linn hired programmers and a University of Glasgow computer science lecturer, David Harland, and built an object-oriented language called LINGO. LINGO was a Smalltalk with some Algol in its syntax, not to be confused with the other Lingo programming language, which wouldn’t come along for another eight years.

sentences.en: So around 1981 Linn hired programmers and a University of Glasgow computer science lecturer, David Harland, and built an object-oriented language called LINGO.
sentences.zh: 因此，1981 年左右，Linn 聘请了程序员和格拉斯哥大学计算机科学讲师 David Harland，并构建了一种名为 LINGO 的面向对象语言。

sentences.en: LINGO was a Smalltalk with some Algol in its syntax, not to be confused with the other Lingo programming language, which wouldn’t come along for another eight years.
sentences.zh: LINGO 是一种 Smalltalk，其语法中包含一些 Algol，不要与其他 Lingo 编程语言混淆，后者还要再过八年才出现。

blocks.html_en: Microcode for a recursive tree-copy instruction. Note the instruction calling itself, something forbidden to a conventional CPU (Source: Pountain, 1988).

sentences.en: Microcode for a recursive tree-copy instruction.
sentences.zh: 递归树复制指令的微代码。

sentences.en: Note the instruction calling itself, something forbidden to a conventional CPU (Source: Pountain, 1988).
sentences.zh: 注意指令调用自身，这是传统 CPU 所禁止的（来源：Pountain，1988）。

blocks.html_en: LINGO worked; but it also ran far too slowly on the VAX to automate anything. Tiefenbrun’s response was pure Tiefenbrun. The hardware was the problem, not the software: so Linn would build hardware.

sentences.en: LINGO worked; but it also ran far too slowly on the VAX to automate anything.
sentences.zh: LINGO 起作用了；但它在 VAX 上运行速度也太慢，无法实现任何自动化。

sentences.en: Tiefenbrun’s response was pure Tiefenbrun.
sentences.zh: 蒂芬布伦的反应是纯粹的蒂芬布伦。

sentences.en: The hardware was the problem, not the software: so Linn would build hardware.
sentences.zh: 硬件才是问题所在，而不是软件：所以 Linn 会构建硬件。

blocks.html_en: While stranded on a delayed train home from a seminar, Harland, and another computer engineer Bruno Beloff, sketched the repartitioning of their prototype into custom chips they christened “Rekursiv,” which is why Harland later joked that “the Rekursiv [was] British Rail’s fault.”

sentences.en: While stranded on a delayed train home from a seminar, Harland, and another computer engineer Bruno Beloff, sketched the repartitioning of their prototype into custom chips they christened “Rekursiv,” which is why Harland later joked that “the Rekursiv [was] British Rail’s fault.”
sentences.zh: 当参加完研讨会被困在晚点回家的火车上时，哈兰德和另一位计算机工程师布鲁诺·贝洛夫 (Bruno Beloff) 描绘了如何将他们的原型重新划分为他们命名为“Rekursiv”的定制芯片，这就是为什么哈兰德后来开玩笑说“Rekursiv 是英国铁路的错”。

blocks.html_en: A few years later, in 1984, Tiefenbrun formed Linn Smart Computing, installed his brother Marcus as managing director and Harland as technical director, and funded it with Linn’s own money plus roughly £10 million from the Department of Trade and Industry.

sentences.en: A few years later, in 1984, Tiefenbrun formed Linn Smart Computing, installed his brother Marcus as managing director and Harland as technical director, and funded it with Linn’s own money plus roughly £10 million from the Department of Trade and Industry.
sentences.zh: 几年后，即 1984 年，Tiefenbrun 成立了 Linn SmartComputing，任命他的兄弟 Marcus 为董事总经理，Harland 为技术总监，并用 Linn 自己的资金加上来自贸易和工业部的大约 1000 万英镑为其提供资金。

blocks.html_en: LSI Logic fabricated the chips. Four gate arrays in 1.5 micron CMOS, 299 pins each, named NUMERIK, LOGIK, OBJEKT and KLOK. A company that sold the Sondek, the Ittok, and the Asak was never going to name the chips anything else.

sentences.en: LSI Logic fabricated the chips.
sentences.zh: LSI Logic 制造了这些芯片。

sentences.en: Four gate arrays in 1.5 micron CMOS, 299 pins each, named NUMERIK, LOGIK, OBJEKT and KLOK.
sentences.zh: 四个 1.5 微米 CMOS 门阵列，每个 299 个引脚，分别命名为 NUMERIK、LOGIK、OBJEKT 和 KLOK。

sentences.en: A company that sold the Sondek, the Ittok, and the Asak was never going to name the chips anything else.
sentences.zh: 一家销售 Sondek、Ittok 和 Asak 的公司绝不会为这些芯片命名任何其他名称。

blocks.html_en: Objects all the way down

sentences.en: Objects all the way down
sentences.zh: 物体一直向下

blocks.html_en: What made the Rekursiv strange was not that it ran object-oriented programs. It was that a programmer – or, come to that, the compiler – could never see an address. Every object carried a 40-bit number issued at creation, and the OBJEKT chip translated numbers to physical locations through a hashed pager table, checking the type and the bounds of every single access in hardware as it went. Run off the end of an array? The machine refused. Forge a reference? The machine refused. Since only OBJEKT ever knew where anything physically lived, objects could be relocated freely without touching a reference, so garbage collection went into the silicon too: a two-space compacting collector that walked the live objects and slid them into the other half of DRAM while execution carried on above, oblivious.

sentences.en: What made the Rekursiv strange was not that it ran object-oriented programs.
sentences.zh: Rekursiv 的奇怪之处并不是它运行面向对象的程序。

sentences.en: It was that a programmer – or, come to that, the compiler – could never see an address.
sentences.zh: 程序员——或者说编译器——永远看不到地址。

sentences.en: Every object carried a 40-bit number issued at creation, and the OBJEKT chip translated numbers to physical locations through a hashed pager table, checking the type and the bounds of every single access in hardware as it went.
sentences.zh: 每个对象都带有一个在创建时发出的 40 位数字，OBJEKT 芯片通过散列分页表将数字转换为物理位置，同时检查硬件中每次访问的类型和边界。

sentences.en: Run off the end of an array?
sentences.zh: 跑出数组末尾？

sentences.en: The machine refused.
sentences.zh: 机器拒绝了。

sentences.en: Forge a reference?
sentences.zh: 伪造参考？

sentences.en: The machine refused.
sentences.zh: 机器拒绝了。

sentences.en: Since only OBJEKT ever knew where anything physically lived, objects could be relocated freely without touching a reference, so garbage collection went into the silicon too: a two-space compacting collector that walked the live objects and slid them into the other half of DRAM while execution carried on above, oblivious.
sentences.zh: 由于只有 OBJEKT 知道任何东西物理存在的位置，因此对象可以在不接触引用的情况下自由地重新定位，因此垃圾收集也进入了芯片：一个两空间压缩收集器，它遍历活动对象并将它们滑入 DRAM 的另一半，同时在上面继续执行，而不会被注意到。

blocks.html_en: Persistence worked the same way. Memory and disk were one object store, and if a needed object wasn’t in DRAM the processor simply stalled, mid-instruction, while an external disk processor fetched it, then carried on as if nothing had happened. Because paging sat below the level of instruction execution, a single microcoded instruction could be arbitrarily complex, and could even call itself. That’s the recursion in the name. There was no fixed instruction set at all; the instruction set was a loadable artefact, and you gave the machine whichever one suited your language. Linn supplied C, James Lothian microcoded a Prolog instruction set, a Manchester group put Scheme on it, and Aberdeen ported the persistent language PS-algol. I am a St Andrews alumnus who was around at the right (or if you want to look at it like that, the wrong) time. I can still remember more than I’d want to about PS-algol. But that, as they say, is altogether another story.

sentences.en: Persistence worked the same way.
sentences.zh: 坚持也有同样的效果。

sentences.en: Memory and disk were one object store, and if a needed object wasn’t in DRAM the processor simply stalled, mid-instruction, while an external disk processor fetched it, then carried on as if nothing had happened.
sentences.zh: 内存和磁盘是一个对象存储，如果所需的对象不在 DRAM 中，处理器就会在指令执行过程中停止，而外部磁盘处理器会获取该对象，然后继续执行，就像什么也没发生一样。

sentences.en: Because paging sat below the level of instruction execution, a single microcoded instruction could be arbitrarily complex, and could even call itself.
sentences.zh: 由于分页位于指令执行级别以下，因此单个微编码指令可以任意复杂，甚至可以调用自身。

sentences.en: That’s the recursion in the name.
sentences.zh: 这就是名字中的递归。

sentences.en: There was no fixed instruction set at all; the instruction set was a loadable artefact, and you gave the machine whichever one suited your language.
sentences.zh: 根本没有固定的指令集；指令集是一个可加载的人工制品，您可以为机器提供适合您的语言的指令集。

sentences.en: Linn supplied C, James Lothian microcoded a Prolog instruction set, a Manchester group put Scheme on it, and Aberdeen ported the persistent language PS-algol.
sentences.zh: Linn 提供了 C，James Lothian 对 Prolog 指令集进行了微编码，曼彻斯特的一个小组在其上添加了Scheme，而 Aberdeen 则移植了持久性语言 PS-algol。

sentences.en: I am a St Andrews alumnus who was around at the right (or if you want to look at it like that, the wrong) time.
sentences.zh: 我是圣安德鲁斯大学的校友，我在正确的时间（或者如果你想这样看的话，是错误的）出现在了正确的时间。

sentences.en: I can still remember more than I’d want to about PS-algol.
sentences.zh: 关于 PS-algol，我仍然记得比我想要的更多的东西。

sentences.en: But that, as they say, is altogether another story.
sentences.zh: 但正如他们所说，那完全是另一回事了。

blocks.html_en: The numbers Linn claimed were spectacular. A CONS cell every two microseconds, twenty times the speed of Lisp on a Symbolics workstation, and Prolog unification as a single instruction. But the figures came from Linn’s own simulations, reported in Byte magazine with the company’s cooperation, and nobody ever reproduced them independently.

sentences.en: The numbers Linn claimed were spectacular.
sentences.zh: 林恩声称的数字非常惊人。

sentences.en: A CONS cell every two microseconds, twenty times the speed of Lisp on a Symbolics workstation, and Prolog unification as a single instruction.
sentences.zh: 每两微秒一个 CONS 单元，是符号工作站上 Lisp 速度的二十倍，并且 Prolog 统一为单个指令。

sentences.en: But the figures came from Linn’s own simulations, reported in Byte magazine with the company’s cooperation, and nobody ever reproduced them independently.
sentences.zh: 但这些数据来自林恩自己的模拟，并与该公司合作在《Byte》杂志上报道，没有人独立复制过它们。

blocks.html_en: The attack of the killer micros

sentences.en: The attack of the killer micros
sentences.zh: 微型杀手的攻击

blocks.html_en: The Rekursiv belonged to a respectable intellectual tradition. Through the seventies the industry consensus held that hardware should rise to meet the language, closing what the literature called the semantic gap; Intel bet its first 32-bit design, the object-oriented iAPX 432, on exactly this, and Symbolics built a workstation business on Lisp in microcode.

sentences.en: The Rekursiv belonged to a respectable intellectual tradition.
sentences.zh: 《Rekursiv》属于受人尊敬的知识传统。

sentences.en: Through the seventies the industry consensus held that hardware should rise to meet the language, closing what the literature called the semantic gap; Intel bet its first 32-bit design, the object-oriented iAPX 432, on exactly this, and Symbolics built a workstation business on Lisp in microcode.
sentences.zh: 整个七十年代，业界一致认为硬件应该迎合语言，缩小文献中所谓的语义鸿沟； Intel 将其第一个 32 位设计、面向对象的 iAPX 432 押注在这一点上，而 Symbolics 在微码 Lisp 上建立了工作站业务。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately for the Rekursiv that tradition was demolished in public at almost the exact moment Linn committed to it. Patterson and Ditzel made the case for RISC in 1980: compilers use a fraction of a complex instruction set, the complexity taxes the common case, and simple instructions plus caches plus good compilers go faster. By 1984 Berkeley’s SOAR project had run Smalltalk, the canonical dynamic object language, fast on a simple chip of thirty-five thousand transistors. The Rekursiv’s central assumption was publicly undercut four years before the first board powered on.

sentences.en: Unfortunately for the Rekursiv that tradition was demolished in public at almost the exact moment Linn committed to it.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，对于 Rekursiv 来说，这一传统几乎在林恩致力于这一传统的同时就被公开摧毁了。

sentences.en: Patterson and Ditzel made the case for RISC in 1980: compilers use a fraction of a complex instruction set, the complexity taxes the common case, and simple instructions plus caches plus good compilers go faster.
sentences.zh: Patterson 和 Ditzel 在 1980 年提出了 RISC 的案例：编译器使用复杂指令集的一小部分，复杂性对常见情况造成负担，简单指令加上高速缓存加上好的编译器会运行得更快。

sentences.en: By 1984 Berkeley’s SOAR project had run Smalltalk, the canonical dynamic object language, fast on a simple chip of thirty-five thousand transistors.
sentences.zh: 到 1984 年，伯克利的 SOAR 项目已经在包含 35000 个晶体管的简单芯片上快速运行了 Smalltalk（一种规范的动态对象语言）。

sentences.en: The Rekursiv’s central assumption was publicly undercut four years before the first board powered on.
sentences.zh: Rekursiv 的核心假设在第一块主板上线四年前就被公开削弱。

blocks.html_en: Then the economics arrived. Commodity microprocessors improved at roughly 52% a year from 1986 to 2003, the period Eugene Brooks of Lawrence Livermore christened the attack of the killer micros. The Rekursiv took four years to design. It was conceived when a VAX defined respectable performance, and in 1988 it emerged into a world of SPARCstations and 386s, with the 486 arriving close behind.

sentences.en: Then the economics arrived.
sentences.zh: 然后经济学就来了。

sentences.en: Commodity microprocessors improved at roughly 52% a year from 1986 to 2003, the period Eugene Brooks of Lawrence Livermore christened the attack of the killer micros.
sentences.zh: 从 1986 年到 2003 年，商用微处理器以每年大约 52% 的速度进步，劳伦斯·利弗莫尔 (Lawrence Livermore) 的尤金·布鲁克斯 (Eugene Brooks) 将这段时期称为“杀手级微处理器”的攻击。

sentences.en: The Rekursiv took four years to design.
sentences.zh: Rekursiv 的设计耗时四年。

sentences.en: It was conceived when a VAX defined respectable performance, and in 1988 it emerged into a world of SPARCstations and 386s, with the 486 arriving close behind.
sentences.zh: 它是在 VAX 定义了令人尊敬的性能时构想出来的，并于 1988 年进入了 SPARCstation 和 386 的世界，486 紧随其后。

blocks.html_en: Lothian, who was there, put it plainly enough five years later: the machine simply couldn’t hold its own against the new workstations. Around twenty or thirty boards were built. Most went to universities. There’s no record that suggests one of them ever ran Linn’s production line, which was the entire point of the exercise. The one field comparison that survives has a threaded-code LINGO on a Sun-3 running about twice as fast as the Rekursiv board it was meant to be inferior to, and which was designed with the intent of running it.

sentences.en: Lothian, who was there, put it plainly enough five years later: the machine simply couldn’t hold its own against the new workstations.
sentences.zh: 五年后，当时在场的洛锡安 (Lothian) 说得很清楚：这台机器根本无法对抗新工作站。

sentences.en: Around twenty or thirty boards were built.
sentences.zh: 大约建造了二十或三十块木板。

sentences.en: Most went to universities.
sentences.zh: 大多数人去了大学。

sentences.en: There’s no record that suggests one of them ever ran Linn’s production line, which was the entire point of the exercise.
sentences.zh: 没有任何记录表明他们中的一个人曾经管理过林恩的生产线，而这正是这次演习的重点。

sentences.en: The one field comparison that survives has a threaded-code LINGO on a Sun-3 running about twice as fast as the Rekursiv board it was meant to be inferior to, and which was designed with the intent of running it.
sentences.zh: 幸存下来的一项现场比较显示，Sun-3 上的线程代码 LINGO 的运行速度大约是 Rekursiv 板的两倍，而 Rekursiv 板本来是要逊于它的，而 Rekursiv 板的设计初衷就是为了运行它。

blocks.html_en: The end was memorably Glaswegian. Black Monday had squeezed Linn’s finances, a venture rescue never closed, and the final straw was a car park. A Linn delivery driver reversed a van into Harland’s Porsche, Tiefenbrun declined to pay for the repair on the grounds that it happened on private ground, and Harland resigned.

sentences.en: The end was memorably Glaswegian.
sentences.zh: 结局是令人难忘的格拉斯哥风格。

sentences.en: Black Monday had squeezed Linn’s finances, a venture rescue never closed, and the final straw was a car park.
sentences.zh: 黑色星期一挤压了林恩的财务状况，风险救援从未结束，最后一根稻草是停车场。

sentences.en: A Linn delivery driver reversed a van into Harland’s Porsche, Tiefenbrun declined to pay for the repair on the grounds that it happened on private ground, and Harland resigned.
sentences.zh: 一名林恩送货司机将一辆货车倒车撞上了哈兰德的保时捷，蒂芬布伦拒绝支付维修费用，理由是此事发生在私人场地，哈兰德辞职了。

blocks.html_en: On his way out he threw his own accumulated hardware and backup media into the Forth and Clyde Canal. The version of the story where the whole product line goes into the water is better; but the truth is good enough.

sentences.en: On his way out he threw his own accumulated hardware and backup media into the Forth and Clyde Canal.
sentences.zh: 在出去的路上，他将自己积累的硬件和备份介质扔进了福斯和克莱德运河。

sentences.en: The version of the story where the whole product line goes into the water is better; but the truth is good enough.
sentences.zh: 全产品线下水的故事版本更好；但事实已经足够了。

blocks.html_en: At least one complete board escaped, and sits today in the Jim Austin Computer Collection near York.

sentences.en: At least one complete board escaped, and sits today in the Jim Austin Computer Collection near York.
sentences.zh: 至少有一块完整的主板逃脱了，今天位于约克附近的吉姆·奥斯汀计算机收藏馆中。

blocks.html_en: Linn, meanwhile, recycled the name Numerik for the DAC in its CD range, which is as good a headstone as any. Harland left computing entirely around 1995 and became one of the most prolific historians of spaceflight, several dozen books and counting. As a recovering astronomer myself, I can only approve of the career change.

sentences.en: Linn, meanwhile, recycled the name Numerik for the DAC in its CD range, which is as good a headstone as any.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，Linn 在其 CD 系列中为 DAC 重新命名为 Numerick，这也是一个很好的墓碑。

sentences.en: Harland left computing entirely around 1995 and became one of the most prolific historians of spaceflight, several dozen books and counting.
sentences.zh: 哈兰德在 1995 年左右完全离开了计算机领域，成为最多产的航天历史学家之一，出版了数十本书并进行了计数。

sentences.en: As a recovering astronomer myself, I can only approve of the career change.
sentences.zh: 作为一名正在康复的天文学家，我只能赞同这种职业转变。

blocks.html_en: Right about everything?

sentences.en: Right about everything?
sentences.zh: 一切都对吗？

blocks.html_en: But here is why this is all more than an historic anecdote. Because I think we need to take a hard look at the Rekursiv’s four defining design decisions, and then ask where each one stands today.

sentences.en: But here is why this is all more than an historic anecdote.
sentences.zh: 但这不仅仅是一个历史轶事。

sentences.en: Because I think we need to take a hard look at the Rekursiv’s four defining design decisions, and then ask where each one stands today.
sentences.zh: 因为我认为我们需要认真审视 Rekursiv 的四个定义性设计决策，然后询问每个决策目前的情况。

blocks.html_en: Hardware memory safety

sentences.en: Hardware memory safety
sentences.zh: 硬件内存安全

blocks.html_en: OBJEKT checked the type and bounds of every access against references the program couldn’t forge. That’s a working description of CHERI, the capability architecture Robert Watson and colleagues have been building at Cambridge and SRI since 2010, in which every pointer carries hardware-enforced bounds, permissions and an unforgeable validity tag.

sentences.en: OBJEKT checked the type and bounds of every access against references the program couldn’t forge.
sentences.zh: OBJEKT 根据程序无法伪造的引用检查每次访问的类型和边界。

sentences.en: That’s a working description of CHERI, the capability architecture Robert Watson and colleagues have been building at Cambridge and SRI since 2010, in which every pointer carries hardware-enforced bounds, permissions and an unforgeable validity tag.
sentences.zh: 这是 CHERI 的工作描述，这是 Robert Watson 及其同事自 2010 年以来一直在剑桥和 SRI 构建的功能架构，其中每个指针都带有硬件强制的边界、权限和不可伪造的有效性标签。

blocks.html_en: Arm built it into the Morello prototype boards it shipped in 2022 under the UK’s Digital Security by Design programme, and ships the lighter-weight Memory Tagging Extension in Android phones today.

sentences.en: Arm built it into the Morello prototype boards it shipped in 2022 under the UK’s Digital Security by Design programme, and ships the lighter-weight Memory Tagging Extension in Android phones today.
sentences.zh: Arm 将其内置到 Morello 原型板上，并根据英国的数字安全设计计划于 2022 年发货，并于今天在 Android 手机中发货了更轻量级的内存标记扩展。

blocks.html_en: Microsoft’s security team concluded in 2020 that CHERI would have deterministically mitigated at least two thirds of the memory-safety vulnerabilities it patched in 2019. The idea that the object, with its type and its bounds, is the proper unit of hardware protection has gone from eccentric to frontier.

sentences.en: Microsoft’s security team concluded in 2020 that CHERI would have deterministically mitigated at least two thirds of the memory-safety vulnerabilities it patched in 2019.
sentences.zh: 微软安全团队在 2020 年得出结论，CHERI 将确定性地缓解其在 2019 年修补的至少三分之二的内存安全漏洞。

sentences.en: The idea that the object, with its type and its bounds, is the proper unit of hardware protection has gone from eccentric to frontier.
sentences.zh: 对象及其类型和边界是硬件保护的适当单元的想法已经从古怪变成了前沿。

blocks.html_en: Garbage collection as an architectural concern

sentences.en: Garbage collection as an architectural concern
sentences.zh: 垃圾收集作为一个建筑问题

blocks.html_en: Azul Systems spent the 2000s selling Vega appliances, custom multicore processors with hardware read-barrier support, to make pause-free collection practical for Java heaps; the C4 collector that came out of that work now runs in pure software on commodity x86. The algorithm survived the move; the custom silicon stayed behind.

sentences.en: Azul Systems spent the 2000s selling Vega appliances, custom multicore processors with hardware read-barrier support, to make pause-free collection practical for Java heaps; the C4 collector that came out of that work now runs in pure software on commodity x86.
sentences.zh: Azul Systems 在 2000 年代销售 Vega 设备、具有硬件读屏障支持的定制多核处理器，使无暂停收集对于 Java 堆变得实用；这项工作产生的 C4 收集器现在在商品 x86 上以纯软件形式运行。

sentences.en: The algorithm survived the move; the custom silicon stayed behind.
sentences.zh: 该算法在迁移后仍然存在。定制硅留在了后面。

blocks.html_en: The single-level persistent store

sentences.en: The single-level persistent store
sentences.zh: 单级持久存储

blocks.html_en: IBM shipped single-level persistent stores in the System/38 in 1978, carried it through the AS/400, and it survives today as IBM i; Intel spent billions trying to reboot the idea as Optane before winding it down in 2022, and the same ambitions are now migrating into CXL memory fabrics. An idea the market keeps finding reasons to want, and reasons not to pay for, is different from a bad idea.

sentences.en: IBM shipped single-level persistent stores in the System/38 in 1978, carried it through the AS/400, and it survives today as IBM i; Intel spent billions trying to reboot the idea as Optane before winding it down in 2022, and the same ambitions are now migrating into CXL memory fabrics.
sentences.zh: IBM 于 1978 年在 System/38 中发布了单级持久存储，并通过 AS/400 进行了延续，至今仍以 IBM i 的形式存在。英特尔花费了数十亿美元试图将这个想法重新命名为 Optane，然后在 2022 年结束它，同样的雄心现在正在迁移到 CXL 内存结构。

sentences.en: An idea the market keeps finding reasons to want, and reasons not to pay for, is different from a bad idea.
sentences.zh: 市场不断寻找想要的理由和不花钱的理由，这个想法与坏想法不同。

blocks.html_en: Silicon shaped to a workload rather than to everything

sentences.en: Silicon shaped to a workload rather than to everything
sentences.zh: 硅是根据工作负载而不是一切来设计的

blocks.html_en: This is the biggest design decision: the one that is now just the way the industry works. Hennessy and Patterson, the two people most responsible for burying machines like the Rekursiv, spent their Turing Lecture arguing that domain-specific architectures are the only road forward now that Dennard scaling is dead and Moore’s Law is wheezing. Or to be more honest, also dead.

sentences.en: This is the biggest design decision: the one that is now just the way the industry works.
sentences.zh: 这是最大的设计决策：现在正是该行业的运作方式。

sentences.en: Hennessy and Patterson, the two people most responsible for burying machines like the Rekursiv, spent their Turing Lecture arguing that domain-specific architectures are the only road forward now that Dennard scaling is dead and Moore’s Law is wheezing.
sentences.zh: Hennessy 和 Patterson 这两个对埋葬 Rekursiv 等机器负有最大责任的人，在他们的图灵讲座中认为，既然登纳德缩放比例已死，摩尔定律已经失效，特定领域的架构是唯一的前进道路。

sentences.en: Or to be more honest, also dead.
sentences.zh: 或者说实话，也死了。

blocks.html_en: Google’s TPU, Groq’s deterministic streaming processor, Cerebras’s wafer-scale engine and Etched’s transformer ASIC are all machines built to run one computational language well; it just happens that the language is now linear algebra rather than Smalltalk.

sentences.en: Google’s TPU, Groq’s deterministic streaming processor, Cerebras’s wafer-scale engine and Etched’s transformer ASIC are all machines built to run one computational language well; it just happens that the language is now linear algebra rather than Smalltalk.
sentences.zh: Google 的 TPU、Groq 的确定性流处理器、Cerebras 的晶圆级引擎和 Etched 的变压器 ASIC 都是为良好运行一种计算语言而构建的机器；只是碰巧现在的语言是线性代数而不是 Smalltalk。

blocks.html_en: Four for four

sentences.en: Four for four
sentences.zh: 四对四

blocks.html_en: Every architectural conviction that went into the canal with the Rekursiv is either shipping, in production, at scale, or is the declared direction of an entire field. Four correct calls in a row is too many for luck. The likelier reading is that the almost forty years in between were not the natural state of computing but an accommodation to a scarcity, and that the scarcity has since gone away.

sentences.en: Every architectural conviction that went into the canal with the Rekursiv is either shipping, in production, at scale, or is the declared direction of an entire field.
sentences.zh: 与 Rekursiv 一起进入运河的每一个建筑信念要么是运输、生产、规模，要么是整个领域的声明方向。

sentences.en: Four correct calls in a row is too many for luck.
sentences.zh: 连续四次正确跟注对于运气来说太多了。

sentences.en: The likelier reading is that the almost forty years in between were not the natural state of computing but an accommodation to a scarcity, and that the scarcity has since gone away.
sentences.zh: 更可能的解读是，这中间的近四十年并不是计算的自然状态，而是对稀缺性的适应，而这种稀缺性已经消失了。

blocks.html_en: I made a different version of this same argument back in June, about software rather than silicon. In my essay a distribution of one I argued that bespoke software was the original, correct arrangement, that fifty years of productised general-purpose software were a compromise forced by the economics of scarce programmers, and that AI has ended the compromise.

sentences.en: I made a different version of this same argument back in June, about software rather than silicon.
sentences.zh: 早在六月，我就同一论点提出了不同版本，关于软件而不是硅。

sentences.en: In my essay a distribution of one I argued that bespoke software was the original, correct arrangement, that fifty years of productised general-purpose software were a compromise forced by the economics of scarce programmers, and that AI has ended the compromise.
sentences.zh: 在我的一篇文章中，我认为定制软件是原始的、正确的安排，五十年的产品化通用软件是稀缺程序员的经济所迫使的妥协，而人工智能已经结束了这种妥协。

blocks.html_en: The general-purpose commodity processor was also a compromise, forced by a 52%-a-year improvement curve that made any bespoke architecture obsolete before it reached silicon. That curve is gone. The compromise is going with it; and the workload-shaped machine, the thing Harland was building in 1984, is what comes back.

sentences.en: The general-purpose commodity processor was also a compromise, forced by a 52%-a-year improvement curve that made any bespoke architecture obsolete before it reached silicon.
sentences.zh: 通用商品处理器也是一种折衷方案，每年 52% 的改进曲线迫使任何定制架构在进入硅片之前就已经过时。

sentences.en: That curve is gone.
sentences.zh: 那个曲线已经消失了。

sentences.en: The compromise is going with it; and the workload-shaped machine, the thing Harland was building in 1984, is what comes back.
sentences.zh: 妥协随之而来； Harland 在 1984 年建造的工作量型机器又回来了。

blocks.html_en: Where the semantics live

sentences.en: Where the semantics live
sentences.zh: 语义所在的地方

blocks.html_en: The comfortable lesson, and the one usually made about the Rekursiv, is that you don’t fight the commodity curve. This is true, but no longer very useful, because the curve is dead. The useful lesson is subtler, and you can see it when you think about why IBM succeeded with the same ideas at the same time.

sentences.en: The comfortable lesson, and the one usually made about the Rekursiv, is that you don’t fight the commodity curve.
sentences.zh: 关于 Rekursiv 的一个令人舒服的教训是，你不要与商品曲线作斗争。

sentences.en: This is true, but no longer very useful, because the curve is dead.
sentences.zh: 这是事实，但不再很有用，因为曲线已经死了。

sentences.en: The useful lesson is subtler, and you can see it when you think about why IBM succeeded with the same ideas at the same time.
sentences.zh: 这个有用的教训更加微妙，当您思考为什么 IBM 同时以相同的想法取得成功时，您就会明白这一点。

blocks.html_en: The System/38 had the Rekursiv’s deepest commitments: single-level store, persistent objects addressed by name rather than address, capabilities, everything an object. It thrived for decades because IBM put those semantics in a virtual instruction set, the Technology Independent Machine Interface (TIMI), and translated to whatever processor was underneath. When the platform moved to PowerPC in 1995, customer applications didn’t even need recompiling. Linn welded the same semantics into four specific gate arrays. The abstraction is still running today; the gate arrays lasted five years.

sentences.en: The System/38 had the Rekursiv’s deepest commitments: single-level store, persistent objects addressed by name rather than address, capabilities, everything an object.
sentences.zh: System/38 具有 Rekursiv 最深刻的承诺：单级存储、按名称而不是地址寻址的持久对象、功能、一切都是对象。

sentences.en: It thrived for decades because IBM put those semantics in a virtual instruction set, the Technology Independent Machine Interface (TIMI), and translated to whatever processor was underneath.
sentences.zh: 它蓬勃发展了数十年，因为 IBM 将这些语义放入虚拟指令集（技术独立机器接口 (TIMI)）中，并转换为底层的任何处理器。

sentences.en: When the platform moved to PowerPC in 1995, customer applications didn’t even need recompiling.
sentences.zh: 当平台于 1995 年迁移到 PowerPC 时，客户应用程序甚至不需要重新编译。

sentences.en: Linn welded the same semantics into four specific gate arrays.
sentences.zh: Linn 将相同的语义焊接到四个特定的门阵列中。

sentences.en: The abstraction is still running today; the gate arrays lasted five years.
sentences.zh: 该抽象至今仍在运行；门阵持续了五年。

blocks.html_en: Interestingly enough, Britain has run this experiment more than once, but with very different outcomes.

sentences.en: Interestingly enough, Britain has run this experiment more than once, but with very different outcomes.
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，英国不止一次进行过这样的实验，但结果却截然不同。

blocks.html_en: Acorn, another small British company with contrarian silicon convictions, designed the ARM 1 in 1985; and the business that conquered the world was not Acorn’s computers but the licensing company that sold the abstraction, an instruction set anyone could implement on whatever process node the commodity curve offered next.

sentences.en: Acorn, another small British company with contrarian silicon convictions, designed the ARM 1 in 1985; and the business that conquered the world was not Acorn’s computers but the licensing company that sold the abstraction, an instruction set anyone could implement on whatever process node the commodity curve offered next.
sentences.zh: Acorn 是另一家拥有逆向硅理念的英国小公司，于 1985 年设计了 ARM 1；征服世界的企业不是 Acorn 的计算机，而是销售抽象的授权公司，这是任何人都可以在商品曲线接下来提供的任何流程节点上实施的指令集。

blocks.html_en: Inmos, meanwhile, welded David May’s genuinely brilliant parallel architecture into the Transputer, was outrun by exactly the same curve that took the Rekursiv, and survives today mainly as lineage: May’s XMOS, and Bristol’s continuing habit of contrarian silicon, through to Graphcore. Put the ideas above the silicon and they can outlive any particular chip. Put them in the silicon and they live exactly as long as the process node does.

sentences.en: Inmos, meanwhile, welded David May’s genuinely brilliant parallel architecture into the Transputer, was outrun by exactly the same curve that took the Rekursiv, and survives today mainly as lineage: May’s XMOS, and Bristol’s continuing habit of contrarian silicon, through to Graphcore.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，Inmos 将 David May 真正出色的并行架构焊接到了 Transputer 中，但被与 Rekursiv 完全相同的曲线所超越，并且主要作为谱系保留至今：May 的 XMOS，以及 Bristol 持续采用逆向硅的习惯，一直到 Graphcore。

sentences.en: Put the ideas above the silicon and they can outlive any particular chip.
sentences.zh: 将想法置于硅之上，它们可以比任何特定芯片更长寿。

sentences.en: Put them in the silicon and they live exactly as long as the process node does.
sentences.zh: 将它们放入硅中，它们的寿命与工艺节点的寿命完全相同。

blocks.html_en: Everyone needs a canal

sentences.en: Everyone needs a canal
sentences.zh: 每个人都需要一条运河

blocks.html_en: The Rekursiv was hardware reshaped to fit a language, because the general-purpose machine fitted the language badly. What’s happening now is the same move in reverse: a whole class of new languages reshaped to fit a machine, because general-purpose languages fit LLMs so poorly. There are enough of them now to fill a catalogue, which I maintain at agentlanguages.dev, where they split into camps that disagree about almost everything except the diagnosis.

sentences.en: The Rekursiv was hardware reshaped to fit a language, because the general-purpose machine fitted the language badly.
sentences.zh: Rekursiv 的硬件经过重新设计以适应某种语言，因为通用机器不太适合这种语言。

sentences.en: What’s happening now is the same move in reverse: a whole class of new languages reshaped to fit a machine, because general-purpose languages fit LLMs so poorly.
sentences.zh: 现在发生的事情是同样的相反的举动：一整类新语言被重塑以适应机器，因为通用语言非常不适合法学硕士。

sentences.en: There are enough of them now to fill a catalogue, which I maintain at agentlanguages.dev, where they split into camps that disagree about almost everything except the diagnosis.
sentences.zh: 现在他们的数量已经足够填满我在 agentlanguages.dev 上维护的目录了，在那里他们分成了不同的阵营，除了诊断之外，他们几乎在所有事情上都存在分歧。

blocks.html_en: Vera, the language I released back in February, is mine. But the instinct underneath all of them is the one Harland was acting on in 1984: that the substrate should change to meet its authors. For him the authors were human and the substrate silicon. This time the authors are models and the substrate the language. Harland made his bet at the worst possible moment in the history of the commodity curve. I’d like to think mine is better timed. The canal is patient either way.

sentences.en: Vera, the language I released back in February, is mine.
sentences.zh: Vera，我在二月份发布的语言，是我的。

sentences.en: But the instinct underneath all of them is the one Harland was acting on in 1984: that the substrate should change to meet its authors.
sentences.zh: 但所有这些背后的本能正是哈兰德在 1984 年所采取的行动：基材应该改变以适应其作者。

sentences.en: For him the authors were human and the substrate silicon.
sentences.zh: 对他来说，作者是人类和衬底硅。

sentences.en: This time the authors are models and the substrate the language.
sentences.zh: 这次作者是模型和语言的基础。

sentences.en: Harland made his bet at the worst possible moment in the history of the commodity curve.
sentences.zh: 哈兰德在商品曲线历史上最糟糕的时刻做出了他的赌注。

sentences.en: I’d like to think mine is better timed.
sentences.zh: 我想我的时机更好。

sentences.en: The canal is patient either way.
sentences.zh: 不管怎样，运河都是有耐心的。

blocks.html_en: The second time around

sentences.en: The second time around
sentences.zh: 第二次

blocks.html_en: Which brings us to why I’m writing about a dead Glaswegian computer.

sentences.en: Which brings us to why I’m writing about a dead Glaswegian computer.
sentences.zh: 这让我们明白了为什么我要写关于一台死掉的格拉斯哥计算机的文章。

blocks.html_en: Our thesis at Negroni has always been that technology waves are cyclical, and that reading the cycle matters more than reading the technology. The Rekursiv is the cleanest case study I know of a team that read the technology almost perfectly and the cycle catastrophically: right about memory safety, right about garbage collection, right about persistence, right about workload-shaped silicon, and wrong about what year it was needed.

sentences.en: Our thesis at Negroni has always been that technology waves are cyclical, and that reading the cycle matters more than reading the technology.
sentences.zh: 我们内格罗尼的论点始终是，技术浪潮是周期性的，解读周期比解读技术更重要。

sentences.en: The Rekursiv is the cleanest case study I know of a team that read the technology almost perfectly and the cycle catastrophically: right about memory safety, right about garbage collection, right about persistence, right about workload-shaped silicon, and wrong about what year it was needed.
sentences.zh: Rekursiv 是我所知道的最干净的案例研究，该团队几乎完美地解读了该技术，但对周期的理解却是灾难性的：关于内存安全的正确性，关于垃圾收集的正确性，关于持久性的正确性，关于工作负载形状的芯片的正确性，以及关于需要哪一年的错误。

blocks.html_en: The team at Linn were insanely early with a design philosophy which was, to put it politely, exotic at the time. It’s only now, all these years later, that we know they were right.

sentences.en: The team at Linn were insanely early with a design philosophy which was, to put it politely, exotic at the time.
sentences.zh: Linn 的团队很早就提出了一种设计理念，礼貌地说，这在当时是一种异国情调。

sentences.en: It’s only now, all these years later, that we know they were right.
sentences.zh: 直到这么多年后的现在，我们才知道他们是对的。

blocks.html_en: Judged on its ideas, the Rekursiv holds up embarrassingly well. It’s only judging the execution which makes every decision look wrong. But being right too early is not a business model.

sentences.en: Judged on its ideas, the Rekursiv holds up embarrassingly well.
sentences.zh: 从其理念来看，Rekursiv 的表现令人尴尬。

sentences.en: It’s only judging the execution which makes every decision look wrong.
sentences.zh: 仅仅评判执行情况会让每个决定看起来都是错误的。

sentences.en: But being right too early is not a business model.
sentences.zh: 但过早做对并不是一种商业模式。

blocks.html_en: The reason to tell the story now is that the cycle has actually turned. The 52% curve that killed every bespoke architecture of the eighties ended twenty years ago; RISC-V and modern tooling have collapsed the cost of taping out something opinionated; and the government money is even flowing again: DTI millions into Glasgow back then, Digital Security by Design millions into Cambridge and Morello today.

sentences.en: The reason to tell the story now is that the cycle has actually turned.
sentences.zh: 现在讲这个故事的原因是周期实际上已经转变了。

sentences.en: The 52% curve that killed every bespoke architecture of the eighties ended twenty years ago; RISC-V and modern tooling have collapsed the cost of taping out something opinionated; and the government money is even flowing again: DTI millions into Glasgow back then, Digital Security by Design millions into Cambridge and Morello today.
sentences.zh: 杀死 80 年代所有定制建筑的 52% 曲线在 20 年前就结束了； RISC-V 和现代工具降低了固执己见的流片成本；政府的资金甚至再次流入：当时 DTI 向格拉斯哥投入了数百万美元，而如今，Digital Security by Design 又向剑桥和莫雷洛投入了数百万美元。

blocks.html_en: For the first time since Harland stood on that canal bank with a cardboard box full of silicon, a small team with a contrarian conviction about what silicon should do is not automatically doomed by the calendar. That’s precisely why Negroni looks at silicon companies, and when we do, the Rekursiv is the checklist I follow. Is the workload broad enough to pay for the transistors? Do the ideas live above the silicon or welded into it, and does the team know what the compilers will do to them?

sentences.en: For the first time since Harland stood on that canal bank with a cardboard box full of silicon, a small team with a contrarian conviction about what silicon should do is not automatically doomed by the calendar.
sentences.zh: 自从哈兰德带着装满硅的纸板箱站在运河岸上以来，这是第一次，一个对硅应该做什么抱有逆向信念的小团队不会自动被日历注定要失败。

sentences.en: That’s precisely why Negroni looks at silicon companies, and when we do, the Rekursiv is the checklist I follow.
sentences.zh: 这正是 Negroni 关注硅公司的原因，当我们这样做时，Rekursiv 就是我遵循的清单。

sentences.en: Is the workload broad enough to pay for the transistors?
sentences.zh: 工作负载是否足以支付晶体管的费用？

sentences.en: Do the ideas live above the silicon or welded into it, and does the team know what the compilers will do to them?
sentences.zh: 这些想法是存在于硅片之上还是焊接到硅片上，团队是否知道编译器会对它们做什么？

blocks.html_en: Somewhere at the bottom of the Forth and Clyde Canal there’s a computer that was right about almost everything. The ideas behind it took almost forty years to make it back to the river bank, but every one of them has made it to shore. This time around, our job needs to be keeping the silicon out of the water.

sentences.zh: 在福斯和克莱德运河底部的某个地方，有一台几乎所有事情都正确的计算机。

sentences.en: The ideas behind it took almost forty years to make it back to the river bank, but every one of them has made it to shore.
sentences.zh: 它背后的想法花了近四十年的时间才回到河岸，但每一个都成功地到达了岸边。

sentences.en: This time around, our job needs to be keeping the silicon out of the water.
sentences.zh: 这一次，我们的工作需要让硅远离水。

content_html_zh:
在福斯和克莱德运河底部的某个地方，埋在淤泥中，有一个定制的硅盒子，几乎所有东西都是正确的。

早在 1988 年，一家苏格兰 hi-fi 公司就推出了一款处理器，该处理器可以检查硬件中每次内存访问的类型和范围，在硅中对其自己的堆进行垃圾收集，并将内存和磁盘视为单个持久对象存储。 这个故事的重述使它成为一种迷人的愚蠢行为，一家制造唱片转盘的公司认为冯·诺依曼是错误的。

但 Rekursiv 的故事不只是令人好奇，因为在它下水近 40 年后，它的想法现在正在从 Arm 的生产芯片中发货；还因为扼杀它的经济刚刚发生了逆转。 尽管这么多年过去了，故事的细节大部分都取决于在场人们的回忆，但事实证明，除了硬件的制造年代之外，这家高保真公司几乎所有事情都是正确的。

一家高保真音响公司制造一台计算机

Linn Products 是 Ivor Tiefenbrun 于 1972 年在格拉斯哥成立的公司，如果您了解它，您就会知道 Sondek LP12，至今仍被其支持者广泛认为是有史以来最好的唱片机。

到了 80 年代初，Linn 在城市南部的 Eaglesham 建造了一家现代化工厂，并在两台 VAX-11/750 和两台 11/780 上开展业务。 但蒂芬布伦开始厌恶他们使用的软件。 他想要一个系统，其中工厂中的每个物理对象，直至经过组装、测试甚至售后的每个单独的转盘，都有一个积累其历史记录的影子软件对象（Pountain，Byte，1988 年 11 月）。

因此，1981 年左右，Linn 聘请了程序员和格拉斯哥大学计算机科学讲师 David Harland，并构建了一种名为 LINGO 的面向对象语言。 LINGO 是一种 Smalltalk，其语法中包含一些 Algol，不要与其他 Lingo 编程语言混淆，后者还要再过八年才出现。

MICRO$COPYTREE: entf 1 pagebus d=ustack
    crtf IDXBADTYPES newtrbr _CONS
    incmsp m.sp' newmptr
    jf MICRO$COPYTREE ldustk d=pgrorr     // the CDR branch
    m.fp 1 uaddbr newmptr
    readustk
    pagebus d=ustack
    idx2 newsr newbr loadaddr
    idxget nocheck incmsp m.sp' newmptr
    jf MICRO$COPYTREE ldustk d=memout     // the CAR branch
    js RTN$CONS
    rtf

递归树复制指令的微代码。 注意指令调用自身，这是传统 CPU 所禁止的（来源：Pountain，1988）。

LINGO 起作用了；但它在 VAX 上运行速度也太慢，无法实现任何自动化。 蒂芬布伦的反应是纯粹的蒂芬布伦。 硬件才是问题所在，而不是软件：所以 Linn 会构建硬件。

当参加完研讨会被困在晚点回家的火车上时，哈兰德和另一位计算机工程师布鲁诺·贝洛夫 (Bruno Beloff) 描绘了如何将他们的原型重新划分为他们命名为“Rekursiv”的定制芯片，这就是为什么哈兰德后来开玩笑说“Rekursiv 是英国铁路的错”。

几年后，即 1984 年，Tiefenbrun 成立了 Linn SmartComputing，任命他的兄弟 Marcus 为董事总经理，Harland 为技术总监，并用 Linn 自己的资金加上来自贸易和工业部的大约 1000 万英镑为其提供资金。

LSI Logic 制造了这些芯片。 四个 1.5 微米 CMOS 门阵列，每个 299 个引脚，分别命名为 NUMERIK、LOGIK、OBJEKT 和 KLOK。 一家销售 Sondek、Ittok 和 Asak 的公司绝不会为这些芯片命名任何其他名称。

物体一直向下

Rekursiv 的奇怪之处并不是它运行面向对象的程序。 程序员——或者说编译器——永远看不到地址。 每个对象都带有一个在创建时发出的 40 位数字，OBJEKT 芯片通过散列分页表将数字转换为物理位置，同时检查硬件中每次访问的类型和边界。 跑出数组末尾？ 机器拒绝了。 伪造参考？ 机器拒绝了。 由于只有 OBJEKT 知道任何东西物理存在的位置，因此对象可以在不接触引用的情况下自由地重新定位，因此垃圾收集也进入了芯片：一个两空间压缩收集器，它遍历活动对象并将它们滑入 DRAM 的另一半，同时在上面继续执行，而不会被注意到。

坚持也有同样的效果。 内存和磁盘是一个对象存储，如果所需的对象不在 DRAM 中，处理器就会在指令执行过程中停止，而外部磁盘处理器会获取该对象，然后继续执行，就像什么也没发生一样。 由于分页位于指令执行级别以下，因此单个微编码指令可以任意复杂，甚至可以调用自身。 这就是名字中的递归。 根本没有固定的指令集；指令集是一个可加载的人工制品，您可以为机器提供适合您的语言的指令集。 Linn 提供了 C，James Lothian 对 Prolog 指令集进行了微编码，曼彻斯特的一个小组在其上添加了Scheme，而 Aberdeen 则移植了持久性语言 PS-algol。 我是圣安德鲁斯大学的校友，我在正确的时间（或者如果你想这样看的话，是错误的）出现在了正确的时间。 关于 PS-algol，我仍然记得比我想要的更多的东西。 但正如他们所说，那完全是另一回事了。

林恩声称的数字非常惊人。 每两微秒一个 CONS 单元，是符号工作站上 Lisp 速度的二十倍，并且 Prolog 统一为单个指令。 但这些数据来自林恩自己的模拟，并与该公司合作在《Byte》杂志上报道，没有人独立复制过它们。

微型杀手的攻击

《Rekursiv》属于受人尊敬的知识传统。 整个七十年代，业界一致认为硬件应该迎合语言，缩小文献中所谓的语义鸿沟； Intel 将其第一个 32 位设计、面向对象的 iAPX 432 押注在这一点上，而 Symbolics 在微码 Lisp 上建立了工作站业务。

不幸的是，对于 Rekursiv 来说，这一传统几乎在林恩致力于这一传统的同时就被公开摧毁了。 Patterson 和 Ditzel 在 1980 年提出了 RISC 的案例：编译器使用复杂指令集的一小部分，复杂性对常见情况造成负担，简单指令加上高速缓存加上好的编译器会运行得更快。 到 1984 年，伯克利的 SOAR 项目已经在包含 35000 个晶体管的简单芯片上快速运行了 Smalltalk（一种规范的动态对象语言）。 Rekursiv 的核心假设在第一块主板上线四年前就被公开削弱。

然后经济学就来了。 从 1986 年到 2003 年，商用微处理器以每年大约 52% 的速度进步，劳伦斯·利弗莫尔 (Lawrence Livermore) 的尤金·布鲁克斯 (Eugene Brooks) 将这段时期称为“杀手级微处理器”的攻击。 Rekursiv 的设计耗时四年。 它是在 VAX 定义了令人尊敬的性能时构想出来的，并于 1988 年进入了 SPARCstation 和 386 的世界，486 紧随其后。

五年后，当时在场的洛锡安 (Lothian) 说得很清楚：这台机器根本无法对抗新工作站。 大约建造了二十或三十块木板。 大多数人去了大学。 没有任何记录表明他们中的一个人曾经管理过林恩的生产线，而这正是这次演习的重点。 幸存下来的一项现场比较显示，Sun-3 上的线程代码 LINGO 的运行速度大约是 Rekursiv 板的两倍，而 Rekursiv 板本来是要逊于它的，而 Rekursiv 板的设计初衷就是为了运行它。

结局是令人难忘的格拉斯哥风格。 黑色星期一挤压了林恩的财务状况，风险救援从未结束，最后一根稻草是停车场。 一名林恩送货司机将一辆货车倒车撞上了哈兰德的保时捷，蒂芬布伦拒绝支付维修费用，理由是此事发生在私人场地，哈兰德辞职了。

在出去的路上，他将自己积累的硬件和备份介质扔进了福斯和克莱德运河。 全产品线下水的故事版本更好；但事实已经足够了。

至少有一块完整的主板逃脱了，今天位于约克附近的吉姆·奥斯汀计算机收藏馆中。

与此同时，Linn 在其 CD 系列中为 DAC 重新命名为 Numerick，这也是一个很好的墓碑。 哈兰德在 1995 年左右完全离开了计算机领域，成为最多产的航天历史学家之一，出版了数十本书并进行了计数。 作为一名正在康复的天文学家，我只能赞同这种职业转变。

一切都对吗？

但这不仅仅是一个历史轶事。 因为我认为我们需要认真审视 Rekursiv 的四个定义性设计决策，然后询问每个决策目前的情况。

硬件内存安全

OBJEKT 根据程序无法伪造的引用检查每次访问的类型和边界。 这是 CHERI 的工作描述，这是 Robert Watson 及其同事自 2010 年以来一直在剑桥和 SRI 构建的功能架构，其中每个指针都带有硬件强制的边界、权限和不可伪造的有效性标签。

Arm 将其内置到 Morello 原型板上，并根据英国的数字安全设计计划于 2022 年发货，并于今天在 Android 手机中发货了更轻量级的内存标记扩展。

微软安全团队在 2020 年得出结论，CHERI 将确定性地缓解其在 2019 年修补的至少三分之二的内存安全漏洞。 对象及其类型和边界是硬件保护的适当单元的想法已经从古怪变成了前沿。

垃圾收集作为一个建筑问题

Azul Systems 在 2000 年代销售 Vega 设备、具有硬件读屏障支持的定制多核处理器，使无暂停收集对于 Java 堆变得实用；这项工作产生的 C4 收集器现在在商品 x86 上以纯软件形式运行。 该算法在迁移后仍然存在。定制硅留在了后面。

单级持久存储

IBM 于 1978 年在 System/38 中发布了单级持久存储，并通过 AS/400 进行了延续，至今仍以 IBM i 的形式存在。英特尔花费了数十亿美元试图将这个想法重新命名为 Optane，然后在 2022 年结束它，同样的雄心现在正在迁移到 CXL 内存结构。 市场不断寻找想要的理由和不花钱的理由，这个想法与坏想法不同。

硅是根据工作负载而不是一切来设计的

这是最大的设计决策：现在正是该行业的运作方式。 Hennessy 和 Patterson 这两个对埋葬 Rekursiv 等机器负有最大责任的人，在他们的图灵讲座中认为，既然登纳德缩放比例已死，摩尔定律已经失效，特定领域的架构是唯一的前进道路。 或者说实话，也死了。

Google 的 TPU、Groq 的确定性流处理器、Cerebras 的晶圆级引擎和 Etched 的变压器 ASIC 都是为良好运行一种计算语言而构建的机器；只是碰巧现在的语言是线性代数而不是 Smalltalk。

四对四

与 Rekursiv 一起进入运河的每一个建筑信念要么是运输、生产、规模，要么是整个领域的声明方向。 连续四次正确跟注对于运气来说太多了。 更可能的解读是，这中间的近四十年并不是计算的自然状态，而是对稀缺性的适应，而这种稀缺性已经消失了。

早在六月，我就同一论点提出了不同版本，关于软件而不是硅。 在我的一篇文章中，我认为定制软件是原始的、正确的安排，五十年的产品化通用软件是稀缺程序员的经济所迫使的妥协，而人工智能已经结束了这种妥协。

通用商品处理器也是一种折衷方案，每年 52% 的改进曲线迫使任何定制架构在进入硅片之前就已经过时。 那个曲线已经消失了。 妥协随之而来； Harland 在 1984 年建造的工作量型机器又回来了。

语义所在的地方

关于 Rekursiv 的一个令人舒服的教训是，你不要与商品曲线作斗争。 这是事实，但不再很有用，因为曲线已经死了。 这个有用的教训更加微妙，当您思考为什么 IBM 同时以相同的想法取得成功时，您就会明白这一点。

System/38 具有 Rekursiv 最深刻的承诺：单级存储、按名称而不是地址寻址的持久对象、功能、一切都是对象。 它蓬勃发展了数十年，因为 IBM 将这些语义放入虚拟指令集（技术独立机器接口 (TIMI)）中，并转换为底层的任何处理器。 当平台于 1995 年迁移到 PowerPC 时，客户应用程序甚至不需要重新编译。 Linn 将相同的语义焊接到四个特定的门阵列中。 该抽象至今仍在运行；门阵持续了五年。

有趣的是，英国不止一次进行过这样的实验，但结果却截然不同。

Acorn 是另一家拥有逆向硅理念的英国小公司，于 1985 年设计了 ARM 1；征服世界的企业不是 Acorn 的计算机，而是销售抽象的授权公司，这是任何人都可以在商品曲线接下来提供的任何流程节点上实施的指令集。

与此同时，Inmos 将 David May 真正出色的并行架构焊接到了 Transputer 中，但被与 Rekursiv 完全相同的曲线所超越，并且主要作为谱系保留至今：May 的 XMOS，以及 Bristol 持续采用逆向硅的习惯，一直到 Graphcore。 将想法置于硅之上，它们可以比任何特定芯片更长寿。 将它们放入硅中，它们的寿命与工艺节点的寿命完全相同。

每个人都需要一条运河

Rekursiv 的硬件经过重新设计以适应某种语言，因为通用机器不太适合这种语言。 现在发生的事情是同样的相反的举动：一整类新语言被重塑以适应机器，因为通用语言非常不适合法学硕士。 现在他们的数量已经足够填满我在 agentlanguages.dev 上维护的目录了，在那里他们分成了不同的阵营，除了诊断之外，他们几乎在所有事情上都存在分歧。

Vera，我在二月份发布的语言，是我的。 但所有这些背后的本能正是哈兰德在 1984 年所采取的行动：基材应该改变以适应其作者。 对他来说，作者是人类和衬底硅。 这次作者是模型和语言的基础。 哈兰德在商品曲线历史上最糟糕的时刻做出了他的赌注。 我想我的时机更好。 不管怎样，运河都是有耐心的。

第二次

这让我们明白了为什么我要写关于一台死掉的格拉斯哥计算机的文章。

我们内格罗尼的论点始终是，技术浪潮是周期性的，解读周期比解读技术更重要。 Rekursiv 是我所知道的最干净的案例研究，该团队几乎完美地解读了该技术，但对周期的理解却是灾难性的：关于内存安全的正确性，关于垃圾收集的正确性，关于持久性的正确性，关于工作负载形状的芯片的正确性，以及关于需要哪一年的错误。

Linn 的团队很早就提出了一种设计理念，礼貌地说，这在当时是一种异国情调。 直到这么多年后的现在，我们才知道他们是对的。

从其理念来看，Rekursiv 的表现令人尴尬。 仅仅评判执行情况会让每个决定看起来都是错误的。 但过早做对并不是一种商业模式。

现在讲这个故事的原因是周期实际上已经转变了。 杀死 80 年代所有定制建筑的 52% 曲线在 20 年前就结束了； RISC-V 和现代工具降低了固执己见的流片成本；政府的资金甚至再次流入：当时 DTI 向格拉斯哥投入了数百万美元，而如今，Digital Security by Design 又向剑桥和莫雷洛投入了数百万美元。

自从哈兰德带着装满硅的纸板箱站在运河岸上以来，这是第一次，一个对硅应该做什么抱有逆向信念的小团队不会自动被日历注定要失败。 这正是 Negroni 关注硅公司的原因，当我们这样做时，Rekursiv 就是我遵循的清单。 工作负载是否足以支付晶体管的费用？ 这些想法是存在于硅片之上还是焊接到硅片上，团队是否知道编译器会对它们做什么？

在福斯和克莱德运河底部的某个地方，有一台几乎所有事情都正确的计算机。 它背后的想法花了近四十年的时间才回到河岸，但每一个都成功地到达了岸边。 这一次，我们的工作需要让硅远离水。
